mnemonics: CIA: Cosets, Identity, Abelian (to remember core group properties)
GROUP: Gathers Rules Operations Under Properties
keyPoints: A group must satisfy closure, associativity, identity, and inverse properties.
An Abelian group is a group where the operation is commutative.
Subgroups are subsets of a group that themselves form a group under the same operation.
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (Abelian). Many important groups, like matrix groups, are not.
Forgetting to check the identity element and inverses are actually in the subgroup when testing for subgroups.
